A committee is a person or combination of persons who
receives contributions or nakes expenditures of $l-,000 or more in
a calendar year. (section 82o13.) itContributionsrr and
'rexpendituresrr are defined in part as payments made for political
purposes. (Sections 82O1L and A2O25. ) A conmittee may contribute
i'ro inore tha; $2500 to an individual candidate. (section 85303.)4
A controlled committee is one which is controlled directly or
indirectly by a candidate or which acts jointly with a candidate
in connection with the rnaking of expenditures. (Section 82016.)
A candidate controls a comrnittee if he, hj-s agent, or any other
cornrnittee he controls has a significant influence on the actions
or decisions of the committee. (Section 82oL6. ) To avoid
violating the transfer prohibition of Section 85304 and the trust
provision of Section 85202, a committee cannot be controlled by
more than one candidate. Accordingty, if your cornmittee is a
controfled committee, it may not accept contributions and make
expenditures on behalf of nore than one candidate, because that
would violate the trust and transfer provisions of Sections 85202
and 85304.
If your committee is not controlled by a candidate, the com-
mittee nay raise InoneY and contribute up to $250.05 each to any
number of candidates.S Ho$/ever, the candidates may not commingle
the contributions they receive in one in one bank account but must
deposit the contribution in their own individual campaign
accounts. The candj-dates may make joint expenditures to promote
their carnpaigns if the expenditures are made from their campaiqn
accounts and if thev each pav a pro-rata share of the expense.
(See Lowenstein Advice Letter, A 90-407r coPY enclosed.)
A committee that is not a controlled committee can make
independent expenditures, rtlndependent expenditure" means an
expenditure made in connection with a cornmunication which
exlressly advocates the election of a clearly identified candidate
but which is not made to or at the behest of the affected
candidate or committee. (Section 82031.) Regulation 18215(b)
(copy enclosed) provides that an expenditure is made at the behest
of a candidate if it is trrnade under the control of, at the
direction of, in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with, or at the request or suggestion of a candidate-rt
Whether a comnittee nakes independent expenditures or contribu-
tions will depend on the specific facts of the situation.

under the Act is $5,000.

controlled committee and (2) whether your corumittee can nake
rr independent expenditures. ri Both depend on the specifics of the

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a committee supporting multiple candidates for the Berkeley Rent Control Board can solicit contributions and make expenditures collectively on behalf of those candidates, or whether each candidate must maintain separate campaign accounts under Proposition 73.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Such joint committee activities are not permitted under Proposition 73. Each candidate must establish a separate campaign bank account and may only have one controlled committee. The permissibility of the committee's activities depends on whether it is a controlled committee and whether it makes independent versus coordinated expenditures, which requires factual analysis of the committee-candidate relationship.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document combines two file numbers (I-90-507 and I-90-557) representing related inquiries from two individuals on behalf of the same committee. No explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers, but the request and advice are clearly structured in the body text. Treated as informal assistance under Regulation 18329(c). Document discusses constitutional concerns regarding Proposition 73 contribution limits declared unconstitutional on September 25, 1990, but notes local ordinances remain applicable.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a committee supporting multiple candidates for the Berkeley Rent Control Board can solicit contributions and make expenditures collectively on behalf of those candidates, or whether each candidate must maintain separate campaign accounts under Proposition 73.

CONCLUSION: Such joint committee activities are not permitted under Proposition 73. Each candidate must establish a separate campaign bank account and may only have one controlled committee. The permissibility of the committee's activities depends on whether it is a controlled committee and whether it makes independent versus coordinated expenditures, which requires factual analysis of the committee-candidate relationship.

embedding.summary: This informal advice letter addresses whether Citizens for Fair Rent Control can collectively solicit and spend campaign funds for multiple Berkeley Rent Control Board candidates, as was done before Proposition 73. The FPPC advises that Proposition 73 requires each candidate to maintain separate campaign accounts and prohibits transfer of funds between candidates or commingling of contributions.
